text:
Every year, as autumn leaves transform the landscape into a symphony of hues, there lives in a small town a boy named Oliver. Oliver loved to collect leaves, not just any leaves—young petals from spring tulips, ancient oaks' russeted bark, the delicate fall snowflake patterns. His favorite was a glossy black; he thought they were sleek and mysterious.

One fall day, Oliver discovered something extraordinary. Amid stacks of fallen leaves, nestled in a little hollow by the old oak near the creek, was a curious object woven from bark and vines. It gleamed under the sun's gentle kiss.

Driven by curiosity, Oliver picked up the craftwork. It seemed alive! With gentle humming sounds, it moved slightly. He figured it must be a sort of bird house—or maybe a dollhouse of some sort. He kept it.

His friendship with the odd thing grew. It would listen to stories about far-off places Oliver knew nothing about. Together they wandered through the woodlands
